div1.entryFree: Auburn, probably == whitish, flaxen: heads some brown, some black, some a. Cor. II, 3, 21 (F1 Abram). her hair is a., mine is perfect yellow, Gentl. IV, 4, 194 (Florio, Ed. 1611: Alburno, a fish called a Blaie or Bleake. Also the white, the sappe or softest part of any timber subject to worm-eating. Also that whitish colour of women's hair which we call an Alburne or Aburne colour).
